Title: Integrative omics analysis reveals the genetic basis of fatty acid composition in Brassica napus seeds

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases oleic acid content and decreases erucic acid content in seeds
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Predicted from integrated GWAS, TWAS, developmental transcriptomics, and POCKET within qFA.A09.1 as a major candidate affecting fatty acid composition. 2. Higher expression is associated with higher C18:1 and lower C22:1 in derived varieties. 3. Overexpression decreased C22:1 and increased C18:1, whereas CRISPR mutants showed the opposite pattern. 4. Transcriptome and metabolite analyses suggest it influences fatty acid composition by modulating energy metabolism during seed development.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seed development; significant association at 20 DAF and 40 DAF, with stronger correlation at 20 DAF
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Resequencing BioProject PRJCA002835; seed transcriptome TWAS BioProject PRJCA002836; time-series seed transcriptome BioProject PRJNA722877; PYRD-related transcriptome BioProject PRJNA1214085
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; native promoter variation analyzed in upstream 2 kb haplotypes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS; TWAS; haplotype-effect analysis; expression-phenotype correlation; CRISPR/Cas9 mutagenesis; overexpression; seed fatty acid profiling by GC; RNA-seq of mutant seeds; subcellular localization; primary metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located in qFA.A09.1, a locus under strong domestication selection. Proposed to affect fatty acid composition indirectly by regulating mitochondrial energy metabolism and inhibiting elongation/conversion of C18:1 toward long-chain unsaturated fatty acids. No evidence reported for interaction with key TFs such as LEC1, LEC2, or WRI1.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for improving high-oleic/low-erucic oil quality; favorable haplotype increased during breeding and can be combined with other favorable loci for rapeseed quality improvement
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed developmental stage (20, 35, 40 DAF); multi-location phenotyping across Wuhan, Ezhou, Chengdu, Hefei, Kunming, and Lanzhou
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant overall growth differences detected in gain- or loss-of-function transgenic plants compared with controls

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases oleic acid and decreases erucic acid in seeds
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as a key candidate from qFA.A08 integration analysis and TWAS/developmental module evidence. 2. Expression positively correlated with C18:1 and negatively correlated with C22:1, especially at 40 DAF. 3. Overexpression reduced C22:1 and increased C18:1, whereas CRISPR mutants showed the opposite effects. 4. The optimal haplotype remains underutilized in breeding populations.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing seed; more significant association at 40 DAF; assigned to C1 or C6 expression module
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Resequencing BioProject PRJCA002835; seed transcriptome TWAS BioProject PRJCA002836; time-series seed transcriptome BioProject PRJNA722877
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; native promoter variation analyzed in upstream 2 kb haplotypes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS support at qFA.A08; TWAS; co-expression/developmental transcriptomics; haplotype analysis; CRISPR/Cas9 mutagenesis; overexpression; seed fatty acid profiling by GC
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of six predicted candidates; among validated genes, favorable haplotype not widely adopted across breeding periods or geographic regions, indicating substantial deployment potential.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising underutilized target for high-oleic/low-erucic rapeseed breeding; useful for haplotype-assisted selection or transgenic/editing strategies
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed developmental stage (20 and 40 DAF); multi-location phenotyping
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant overall growth differences detected in transgenic plants

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively modulates oleic acid and negatively modulates erucic acid
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Predicted from integrated multi-omics within qFA.A08 as a key candidate. 2. Showed the strongest expression correlation with C18:1 and C22:1 among six candidates. 3. CRISPR mutants increased C22:1 and decreased C18:1, validating its positive role in favorable fatty acid composition. 4. Favorable haplotype frequency increased during breeding and became predominant in many accessions.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing seed; significant at 20 and 40 DAF
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Resequencing BioProject PRJCA002835; seed transcriptome TWAS BioProject PRJCA002836; time-series seed transcriptome BioProject PRJNA722877
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter variation analyzed in upstream 2 kb haplotypes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS support at qFA.A08; developmental transcriptomics; expression-phenotype correlation; haplotype analysis; CRISPR/Cas9 mutagenesis; seed fatty acid profiling by GC
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A frameshift mutation in exon was detected among natural variants, suggesting major protein functional alteration. Validated only by mutant analysis in this study; no overexpression lines reported.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for high-oleic/low-erucic breeding; favorable haplotype already enriched and can support marker-assisted or genome-editing improvement
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed developmental stage (20 and 40 DAF); multi-location phenotyping
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant overall growth differences detected in mutant plants

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively modulates oleic acid and negatively modulates erucic acid
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified within qFA.C02 and a seed developmental expression module, with expression patterns resembling seed coat development and lipid synthesis genes. 2. Expression positively correlated with C18:1 and negatively correlated with C22:1, especially at 20 DAF. 3. CRISPR mutants increased C22:1 and decreased C18:1. 4. Favorable haplotype increased over breeding periods but is less frequent in North America and Oceania.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Developing seed; member of C3 module; stronger correlation at 20 DAF
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Resequencing BioProject PRJCA002835; seed transcriptome TWAS BioProject PRJCA002836; time-series seed transcriptome BioProject PRJNA722877
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter variation analyzed in upstream 2 kb haplotypes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: GWAS support at qFA.C02; developmental transcriptomics; haplotype analysis; expression-phenotype correlation; CRISPR/Cas9 mutagenesis; seed fatty acid profiling by GC
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate inferred partly from co-expression with seed coat and lipid-synthesis genes; validated only by mutant analysis in this study; no overexpression lines reported.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for improving rapeseed oil quality and regional haplotype optimization
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed developmental stage (20 and 40 DAF); multi-location phenotyping
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant overall growth differences detected in mutant plants
